title: Public read-only homepage/search surface
description: Build a public, unauthenticated homepage with its own reviewed query path (not a direct reuse of the authenticated dashboard query) that lets anonymous users search and view a limited set of current listings and click through via the Phase 1 redirect.
acceptance_criteria: A public homepage route serves search/browse results without requiring login
The public query path is implemented separately from _dashboard_data/_project_detail_data, not a conditional branch inside them
Public listing cards link out via /out/<listing_id> (FEATURE-1010), recording clicks with user_id NULL for anonymous visitors
The public surface shows a limited result set (exact limiting rule documented, e.g. no private/owned projects, no per-user saved data)
Page load and search work with no session/cookie required beyond what Flask needs functionally
notes: See docs/adr/0005-public-vs-signed-in-experience-split.md. Depends on EPIC-103 (ownership model) existing so 'what's private' is well-defined, and EPIC-101 (redirect) for click-through.
